system.$description: General-purpose system accent color. Both link and the focus ring point here, so the binding constraint is the stricter of the two: link is text and needs WCAG 2.2 AA 4.5:1 against every elevation surface it can sit on (all white in light), while the ring needs 3:1 (SC 1.4.11 / focus-ring.md). The previous value, iOS system blue #007AFF, measured 4.02:1 against white and failed AA for the link. 600 is the light theme's interactive calibration (bg and border both sit at 600), and blue.600 clears 6.49:1. 500 (5.38:1) also passes if a lighter accent is preferred; the floor is what CI enforces, the exact rung is a seed.

surface.$description: Raised surface (card/panel). Light: white; depth comes from shadow.

border.$description: Neutral border, for the resting outline of inputs and cards. Its own authored rung, not a gray alias: the gray staircase jumps from 400 (2.47:1 on white) to 500 (5.37:1), and neither lands near the 3:1 that WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.11 asks of a component boundary. #8592A2 is 3.17:1 against white (every light elevation surface is white), the lightest value that still clears the bar. Same principle as the semantic borders (their own rung, calibrated to the ground). Provisional (2026-07); the palette-wide author will revisit.

shadow-umbra.$description: The near, hard shadow. Light draws depth with shadows over an untinted surface, so this is the heavier of the two. Elevation's geometry points at this colour; the colour is a theme's to set, the geometry is not.

shadow-penumbra.$description: The far, soft shadow. Lighter and wider than the umbra.

scrim.$description: Modal scrim base color (opaque). The veil applies it at ~0.4 opacity via the `scrim` token.

focus-ring.$description: Global focus ring color. Defaults to app.system; override per theme.

border.$description: The component's outline, for `outlined` (emphasis.md). Its own rung, not an alias of `bg`: the two answer different questions. `bg` is calibrated to carry white text; a border is calibrated to be visible against the ground plane (WCAG 1.4.11, 3:1). Light's elevation surfaces are all #FFFFFF, so 600 clears it everywhere at 6.45:1. The calibration is kept separate anyway, because dark's cannot be.

scrim.$description: Veil behind modal / dialog / drawer. Stacking order is --layer-scrim; this token carries the visual value only (elevation.md §6). Opacity is a seed; 0.4 leans to the revealing side so the user keeps context of what's behind the modal (Art.1, elevation.md §6).
